text: {"claims":[{"statement":"People with non-specific diabetes eating the highest number of eggs had an 83% increased risk of cardiovascular disease compared with the lowest consumers.","claimType":"empirical","quote":"people with non-specific ‘diabetes’ eating the highest number of eggs had an 83% increased risk of CVD compared with the lowest consumers","entailment":"The quoted sentence directly reports the relative risk increase for high egg consumers versus low consumers in a diabetic population.","population":"people with non-specific diabetes","intervention":"high egg consumption","comparator":"lowest egg consumption","outcome":"cardiovascular disease risk","timeframe":"","quantifiers":"83% increased risk","modality":"associational"},{"statement":"For every 4 eggs per week increment, the relative risk of cardiovascular disease was 40% greater.","claimType":"empirical","quote":"for every 4 egg/week increment, the relative risk was 40% greater","entailment":"The quote specifies a dose‑response relationship indicating a 40% higher relative risk per 4‑egg weekly increase.","population":"","intervention":"increase of 4 eggs per week","comparator":"","outcome":"cardiovascular disease relative risk","timeframe":"","quantifiers":"40% greater relative risk per 4‑egg/week increment","modality":"associational"},{"statement":"Another systematic review and meta‑analysis examining risk of coronary heart disease found no significant association even at intakes of 7 or more eggs per week.","claimType":"empirical","quote":"another SRMA examining risk of CHD found no significant association, even at intakes of 7+ eggs per week","entailment":"The quoted sentence reports a null finding for CHD risk at high egg intake levels.","population":"","intervention":"≥7 eggs per week","comparator":"","outcome":"coronary heart disease risk","timeframe":"","quantifiers":"no significant association","modality":"associational"},{"statement":"Evidence from randomized controlled trials suggests that eggs tend to have overall small effects on blood cholesterol levels.","claimType":"empirical","quote":"evidence from RCT suggests that eggs tend to have overall small effects on blood cholesterol levels","entailment":"The quote conveys that RCT evidence indicates only modest changes in cholesterol from egg consumption.","population":"","intervention":"egg consumption","comparator":"","outcome":"blood cholesterol levels","timeframe":"","quantifiers":"overall small effects","modality":"associational"},{"statement":"Observational studies show a modest beneficial association or no association between egg consumption and cardiovascular disease risk in healthy baseline populations.","claimType":"empirical","quote":"eggs have a modest beneficial association or no association with CVD risk","entailment":"The quoted phrase describes the observed relationship in healthy groups, indicating either a small benefit or no effect.","population":"healthy baseline populations","intervention":"egg consumption","comparator":"","outcome":"cardiovascular disease risk","timeframe":"","quantifiers":"modest beneficial association or no association","modality":"associational"},{"statement":"Observational studies show that in populations with pre‑existing diabetes, higher egg consumption is associated with greater cardiovascular disease risk at higher intakes.","claimType":"empirical","quote":"eggs are associated with greater CVD risk at higher intakes","entailment":"The quote indicates a positive association between higher egg intake and increased CVD risk among diabetics.","population":"populations with pre‑existing diabetes","intervention":"higher egg consumption","comparator":"","outcome":"cardiovascular disease risk","timeframe":"","quantifiers":"greater CVD risk at higher intakes","modality":"associational"},{"statement":"The highest egg consumers had a 68% increased risk of developing type 2 diabetes compared with the lowest consumers.","claimType":"empirical","quote":"High consumers had 68% increased risk of developing T2D","entailment":"The quoted statement reports the relative risk increase for the highest versus lowest egg consumers.","population":"","intervention":"high egg consumption","comparator":"low egg consumption","outcome":"type 2 diabetes incidence","timeframe":"","quantifiers":"68% increased risk","modality":"associational"},{"statement":"The highest egg consumers had a 42% increased risk of developing type 2 diabetes compared with the lowest consumers.","claimType":"empirical","quote":"High consumers had 42% increased risk of T2D","entailment":"The quoted sentence provides the relative risk increase for high versus low egg intake.","population":"","intervention":"high egg consumption","comparator":"low egg consumption","outcome":"type 2 diabetes incidence","timeframe":"","quantifiers":"42% increased risk","modality":"associational"},{"statement":"The highest egg consumers had a 9% increased risk of developing type 2 diabetes compared with the lowest consumers.","claimType":"empirical","quote":"High consumers had 9% increased risk of developing T2D","entailment":"The quote states a modest relative risk increase for the highest egg consumers.","population":"","intervention":"high egg consumption","comparator":"low egg consumption","outcome":"type 2 diabetes incidence","timeframe":"","quantifiers":"9% increased risk","modality":"associational"},{"statement":"Each additional serving of 4 eggs per week was associated with a 29% greater risk of developing non‑specific diabetes.","claimType":"empirical","quote":"finding a 29% greater risk of developing non-specific diabetes for each additional serving of 4 eggs/week","entailment":"The quoted phrase describes a dose‑response increase in diabetes risk per 4‑egg weekly serving.","population":"","intervention":"each additional 4 eggs per week","comparator":"","outcome":"non‑specific diabetes risk","timeframe":"","quantifiers":"29% greater risk per 4‑egg/week serving","modality":"associational"},{"statement":"Each additional egg per day was associated with a 7–13% increased risk of type 2 diabetes.","claimType":"empirical","quote":"each egg per day was associated with a 7–13% increased risk of T2D","entailment":"The quote reports a range of relative risk increase per daily egg added.","population":"","intervention":"each additional egg per day","comparator":"","outcome":"type 2 diabetes risk","timeframe":"","quantifiers":"7–13% increased risk per egg per day","modality":"associational"},{"statement":"Type 2 diabetes risk was elevated by 7% only for egg consumption greater than 4 eggs per week.","claimType":"empirical","quote":"T2D risk was elevated by 7% but only for >4 eggs/week","entailment":"The quoted sentence specifies a conditional increase in risk when intake exceeds 4 eggs per week.","population":"","intervention":">4 eggs per week","comparator":"","outcome":"type 2 diabetes risk","timeframe":"","quantifiers":"7% elevated risk","modality":"conditional"},{"statement":"Intakes of two or more eggs per week were associated with an 11–27% increased risk of type 2 diabetes compared with no egg consumption.","claimType":"empirical","quote":"≥2 eggs/week were associated with an 11–27% increased risk of T2D compared with non-consumption","entailment":"The quote indicates a relative risk increase for moderate egg intake versus zero intake.","population":"","intervention":"≥2 eggs per week","comparator":"no egg consumption","outcome":"type 2 diabetes risk","timeframe":"","quantifiers":"11–27% increased risk","modality":"associational"},{"statement":"Higher egg consumption was associated with a significant 8% reduction in risk of metabolic syndrome.","claimType":"empirical","quote":"higher egg consumption was associated with a significant 8% reduction in risk of MetS","entailment":"The quoted sentence directly reports a protective association between egg intake and metabolic syndrome risk.","population":"young to middle aged participants (331,667)","intervention":"higher egg consumption","comparator":"","outcome":"metabolic syndrome risk","timeframe":"","quantifiers":"8% reduction","modality":"associational"}]}
